mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series signals, with interaction term coefficients statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns during early expansion phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative in over 60 percent of observations when INDPRO rises during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth expands corporate revenue baselines and improves earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions show zero correlation with 60-day forward returns, and the signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL monthly growth surpasses 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative information coefficient over rolling 2-year windows, or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, which triggers negative short-term S&P 500 returns during housing market corrections.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 55 percent of observations when HOUST declines during identified housing correction periods.
